channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: GPT 5.6 SOL: TBH, IT'S OKAY.. I have SERIOUS CONCERNS.
item.description: AICodeKing: AICodeKing reviews GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna, finding them competent but not revolutionary. The author notes mixed benchmark results, underperformance of Luna, concerning cyber capabilities despite OpenAI's safety claims, and questions whether these represent meaningful advances over GPT-5.5.

item.title: Automating workflows and file organization with Antigravity
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Antigravity is a tool that automates file organization and software development workflows using time-based tagging and file system automation. The tool helps developers manage GitHub repositories and local files across multiple projects, reducing search time and improving development efficiency.

item.title: GPT 5.6 banned, Fable banned… it’s actually over.
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej discusses the banning of GPT 5.6 and Fable, asserting that "it's actually over." The claim appears to reference regulatory or policy decisions restricting access to or deployment of these AI systems.

item.title: Use These 17 Claude Plugins, It Will Make You 10x Better.
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI reviews 17 Claude plugins, CLIs, and skills organized by category: design, productivity, and data. The video covers tools like Playwright CLI, Firecrawl, GitHub CLI, and others that integrate with Claude Code to enhance development workflows.

item.title: Stop Writing Tone Instructions. Layer Them. - Isadora Martin-Dye, Isadora & Co
item.description: AI Engineer: This talk presents a layered approach to maintaining brand voice in AI systems, breaking voice into four components: immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and deterministic post-generation veto. The approach draws on production experience from wedding venue, personal companion, and missing persons applications to explain why single-pass prompt instructions fail at scale.

item.title: The hidden system behind every Renaissance trial - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel interviews historian Ada Palmer about legal systems and hidden structures in Renaissance trials. This is a historical discussion that does not directly address AI industry topics.

item.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
item.description: AWS Developers: AWS tutorial introduces a framework for system design interviews using seven steps: requirements gathering, traffic estimation, high-level component design, API contracts, tech stack selection, and implementation constraints. The approach emphasizes problem-first thinking rather than technology-first design.

item.title: Big Technology AI Summit (full): Greg Brockman, Mike Krieger, Aaron Levie & Friends of The Podcast
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Multi-speaker panel at the Big Technology AI Summit (June 2026) discussing the federal recall of Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models, AI infrastructure buildout, enterprise ROI expectations, and competitive dynamics between US and Chinese AI development. Speakers include leaders from OpenAI, Anthropic, Box, Meta, and major tech publications.

item.title: What does the next training paradigm look like?
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel explores what the next training paradigm after scaling laws might look like, covering research bets from major labs. The discussion addresses key challenges including grindability, reinforcement learning from verification, and mechanisms for integrating learning signals back into model weights.

item.title: TEN Framework: Voice AI That Can Actually Be Interrupted
item.description: Better Stack: TEN Framework is an open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents that handle streaming audio, interruptions, VAD, turn detection, STT, LLMs, TTS, avatars, telephony, and multimodal workflows. It uses graph-based extensions and real-time audio pipelines to support natural conversational interactions with proper interruption handling and turn detection.

item.title: 5 things every AI agent harness needs
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech presents five critical components needed in AI agent harnesses to move from prototype to production. The video emphasizes that the model is only the brain and infrastructure around it determines whether agents ship successfully.

item.title: Gemini Canvas Tutorial: How to Use Google's Most Underrated AI Tool
item.description: AI Master: Gemini Canvas is a Google feature integrated into Gemini that generates working websites, no-code apps, dashboards, and audio podcasts from natural language prompts. The tutorial demonstrates six levels of functionality from document editing through advanced multi-tool combinations.

item.title: Learn how Professor David J. Malan realized computer science is the field for him.
item.description: CS50: Professor David J. Malan shares his journey realizing computer science was the right field for him. This video is part of CS50, Harvard University's foundational computer science course offered through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and OpenCourseWare.

item.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
item.description: AI Engineer: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present a system for converting a large personal knowledge vault into actionable context for AI agents. The approach uses a three-layer architecture (raw content, indexed layer, wiki layer) to efficiently organize thousands of notes and documents, enabling deep research queries across personal and web sources without requiring vector or graph databases.

item.title: Ornith (35B,9B) + Hermes,Zed: THE FULLY PRIVATE LOCAL AGENT is ACTUALLY HERE!
item.description: AICodeKing: Ornith is a family of open-source agentic coding models from DeepReinforce, available in sizes from 9B to 397B parameters and built on Gemma and Qwen. The models are designed for coding, tool use, and agentic workflows, with versions available in GGUF format for local deployment.

item.title: How to Create a Client Pitch With AI — From Prompt to PDF in 15 Minutes
item.description: AI Master: Venngage AI is a tool for generating one-pagers and marketing documents from text prompts, automating content, design, visuals, and PDF export in a single workflow. The video demonstrates creating a client-ready pitch document entirely through AI-assisted design.

item.title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
item.description: AI Engineer: Gabe De Mesa from OpenGov discusses building and scaling the OG Assist AI agent product serving thousands of state and local governments. The talk covers agent architecture, core loops, feedback mechanisms, long context handling, monitoring, tooling, and integration with Claude and Cursor.

item.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
item.description: AI Engineer: Victor Savkin argues that current coding agents lack sufficient context and memory to be effective, using an analogy of John Carmack unable to see the full codebase. He frames this as a critical limitation that needs solving and positions Nx and Polygraph as solutions for improving agent autonomy in monorepo environments.

item.title: Fable 5 Returns... This Is Big
item.description: AI Revolution: A video essay claims that Anthropic's Fable 5 model, previously pulled for national security reasons, may be returning based on leaked Claude code strings, Amazon Bedrock listing, Congressional activity, and Trump administration relations. The author argues the ban accelerated development of a stronger successor (Mythos) and that re-release could come with government oversight and revised pricing.

item.title: The Log Is The Agent - Ishaan Sehgal, Omnara
item.description: AI Engineer: Ishaan Sehgal argues that as LLMs commoditize, the true differentiator for agent systems is not frameworks or tools but the session log as a persistent data primitive. He contends that durable, portable logs that make agents independent of specific models or runtimes will become the foundation for next-generation agent infrastructure, enabling durability, continuity, forkability, and portability.

item.title: Recursive Coding Agents - Raymond Weitekamp, OpenProse
item.description: AI Engineer: Recursive Language Models represent an inference-time compute paradigm that applies recursive principles to coding agents for improved performance and reliability. The presentation demonstrates how RLM concepts can be mapped onto coding agent architectures, including comparison with Claude Code sub-agents, and shows concrete applications in tools like Claude Code Dynamic Workflows and OpenProse for solving complex tasks with declarable outcomes.

item.title: When Forgiveness Was a Trap - Ada Palmer
item.description: Dwarkesh Patel: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Ada Palmer on the historical and philosophical dynamics of forgiveness, exploring how forgiveness functioned as a social mechanism that could entrap or constrain actors. Palmer examines forgiveness not as purely ethical but as a complex social and historical phenomenon.

item.title: GTA VI + GPT-6: Rockstar's AI Mechanics Revealed
item.description: AI Master: A YouTube video analyzing GTA VI's actual AI mechanics by examining Rockstar's patents and job postings, debunking viral rumors about GPT-6 integration. The creator argues Rockstar developed a proprietary real-time behavioral simulation system for NPCs rather than relying on OpenAI's models, and provides guidance on distinguishing authentic leaks from false claims.

item.title: What's new with data agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech presented updates on data analytics agents with new capabilities for data engineering, science, and conversational analytics. New developer tools including the Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Model Context Protocol (MCP) integrations enable building custom agents grounded in enterprise data, accessible via Gemini CLI, Claude Code, Codex, and IDEs like VSCode.

item.title: Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
item.description: AI Engineer: Nishant Gupta from Meta discusses production evaluation approaches for autonomous AI agents that go beyond offline benchmarks. The talk covers continuous evaluation pipelines, online vs offline evaluation architectures, human-in-the-loop systems, and methods for detecting drift and hallucinations in agentic workflows at scale.

item.title: Build Systems, Not Code - Angie Jones, Agentic AI Foundation
item.description: AI Engineer: Angie Jones argues that AI coding agents shift engineering focus upward from line-by-line code writing to system design. She contends that traditional engineering disciplines like decomposition, state management, and systems thinking remain central to building agentic systems, and that developer value migrates to the architectural level rather than disappearing.

item.title: Anthropic's Labs Lead On Fable's Capabilities + Building AI-Native Products — With Mike Krieger
item.description: Alex Kantrowitz: Mike Krieger, head of Anthropic Labs, discusses the company's approach to AI-native product development and safety in the context of government intervention forcing Anthropic to pull frontier models Fable and Mythos from the market. Krieger describes how AI tooling accelerates his development workflow and defends Anthropic's safety messaging as substantive rather than promotional.

item.title: Power intelligent agents with AI-native databases
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud Tech argues that AI-native databases are essential for powering intelligent agents with enterprise data. The video explores how such databases enable relevant context, multimodal queries, graph RAG, and integration with standards like Model Context Protocol to streamline agent deployment.

item.title: Boost AI context with hybrid search in Spanner
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented hybrid search capabilities in Spanner that combine full-text and vector search for AI-powered retrieval-augmented generation applications. The tool integrates with Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform to enable contextual, multi-model search over operational data.

item.title: Agent development and AgentOps with BigQuery, ADK, and MCP
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented methods for building agents using the Agent Development Kit (ADK) integrated with Model Context Protocol (MCP) to standardize agent connections to data sources like BigQuery, AlloyDB, and Google Maps. The session covered AgentOps for monitoring agent performance and costs through observability.

item.title: Build connected AI: Orchestrate tools and agents with registries and ADK
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud's Agent Development Kit enables building production agents that access enterprise data across SaaS and on-premises systems. The toolkit uses Agent Registry to securely connect agents to other agents and tools, and supports Model Context Protocol servers for standardized tool integration.

item.title: What's new with Gemini from Google DeepMind
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind presented upgrades to Gemini 3, including improved model reasoning, expanded context window processing, and enhanced multimodal tool use. The presentation focused on enabling developers to build agentic applications using Gemini through Vertex AI and shared research advancements in agent design.

item.title: Building an AI app: A low-code guide for small teams
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud offers a low-code platform for building AI applications by combining corporate data with cloud databases and Gemini. The service enables rapid development of intelligent features like natural language interfaces, hybrid search, image search, and recommendations without requiring deep AI expertise.

item.title: What's new in Looker: Empowering business users in the governed agentic era
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud unveiled advancements to Looker that integrate Gemini AI grounded in a governed semantic layer for business intelligence. New capabilities include dashboard agents that automate workflows, conversational analytics, and embedded analytics for the AI-first enterprise.

item.title: NoSQL for modern apps and AI: The future of Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud presented updates to its NoSQL database services Memorystore, Firestore, and Bigtable, highlighting their capabilities for AI and modern applications. The session covered product releases, architectural innovations, and roadmaps tailored for agentic and AI workloads at scale.

item.title: Implementing DeepMind innovation: Deep research API
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google DeepMind has developed the Deep research API, which enables the creation of agents powered by Gemini to perform comprehensive research tasks. The API allows developers to implement these DeepMind-developed techniques in their applications through Google Cloud.

item.title: What's new in Cloud Run
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced new Cloud Run capabilities designed for deploying AI agent applications and inference workloads with improved scalability and reduced operational overhead. The platform introduces serverless features that address the deployment needs of AI-driven development.

item.title: What's new in Cloud SQL: Drive performance, high availability, and security
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced new AI-driven capabilities in Cloud SQL for MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server, including automated health management and fleet-wide insights. The update also highlights Database Migration Service for low-risk cloud migration and improvements in performance, availability, and security.

item.title: What's new in Google Cloud's agent platform
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud announced an updated agent platform featuring unified Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform with Agent Builder, no-code/low-code Agent Designer, and an Agent Registry for reusable components. The platform includes enterprise governance, management capabilities, time-travel debugging, integrated evaluation, simulation, and expanded Agent Development Kit with agent-to-agent communication and secure code execution.

item.title: The Miranda Hypothesis: How Hamilton Poisoned Persona Evals - Jacob E. Thomas, Results Gen
item.description: AI Engineer: Jacob E. Thomas argues that standard persona evaluation benchmarks fail to detect a failure mode called "Miranda distortion," where AI characters speak from cultural composites in training data rather than from primary documentary records. He proposes shifting from cognitive to epistemic simulation evaluation using domain expert raters, temporal grounding, and the pre-registered Prism Experiment to detect anachronism and documentary consistency instead of just fluency.

item.title: What's new for AI on GKE: Training, serving, and agents
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Kubernetes Engine has added innovations for AI training, serving, and agentic workflows. The platform now supports reinforcement learning and complex AI workloads with improved reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness.

item.title: Engineering the future of Kubernetes for AI at scale
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud discusses Kubernetes evolution for AI-scale workloads, emphasizing hardware-aware platforms, infrastructure elasticity, agentic workflow security, and resource optimization. Google frames itself as the leading Kubernetes contributor steering industry direction for 11 consecutive years.

item.title: Unsure how to approach CS50? Carter Zenke answers.
item.description: CS50: CS50 is Harvard University's introduction to computer science and programming available through multiple platforms including edX, Harvard Extension School, and Harvard Summer School. The course is led by David J. Malan and has an active global community across numerous social media and collaboration platforms.

item.title: Kimi K2.7 Code: THIS is VERY CRAZY MAN!
item.description: AICodeKing: Moonshot AI released Kimi K2.7 Code, an open-source agentic model with one trillion parameters using Mixture-of-Experts architecture. The tool includes a CLI for repository exploration and code editing, supports 256K context windows, and offers subscription plans with predictable pricing for coding tasks.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 3 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 held its final day of main-stage programming on July 2, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco. The livestream featured keynotes, talks, and a startup battlefield competition, serving as a major industry gathering point.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 2 Livestream
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 is a conference held at Moscone West in San Francisco on July 1, 2026. The event features keynote sessions and main-stage programming throughout the day, livestreamed online.

item.title: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 Day 1: Software Factories & Keynotes
item.description: AI Engineer: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 took place June 30, 2026 at Moscone West in San Francisco. Day 1 featured keynote sessions and main-stage programming focused on software factories and AI engineering practices.

item.title: Anthropic accuses Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities - BBC
item.description: Google News AI: Anthropic has accused Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities, according to a BBC report. The accusation suggests unauthorized access to or replication of AI model functionality.

item.title: Ask HN: Where is our profession (programmer) going?
item.description: syntaxbush: A programmer observes that at a visited software company, LLMs like Claude have become the primary tool for code generation and problem-solving, with human code review, abstraction practices, and deep problem understanding being de-emphasized. The author expresses concern that this represents a shift from precise, knowledge-intensive work toward probabilistic, outsourced decision-making.

item.title: The Agentic OS Setup That Will 10x Claude Code
item.description: Chase AI: Chase AI argues that mastering Agentic OS fundamentals improves Claude Code outputs through better loop engineering, memory management, and skills development. An Agentic OS provides customization, visual interfaces, and shareable packages for non-technical teams beyond native Claude Code capabilities.
